question_text: Which of the following is a physical change?

options.label: A
options.text: Burning wood to produce ash and smoke

options.label: C
options.text: Rusting of iron in moist air

options.label: D
options.text: Cooking an egg until it becomes solid

explanation: Melting is a physical change because the water molecules remain the same—only the state changes from solid to liquid. Burning, rusting, and cooking are all chemical changes because they create completely new substances.
